independent_of.note: This module never imports or reads the identity-crosswalk module or artifact, and no availability status is conditioned on identity-resolution status.

mirror_source_commit_pinned_in_artifact.note: the commit that merged PR #159 (Lane A)

validator_enforcement.contract_pins: kind/schema_version/issue/governing_design (readiness-design issue/PR/merge-commit/document paths)/season match pinned constants exactly; top-level artifact fields must be exactly an enumerated closed set
validator_enforcement.mirror_dereferencing: mirror_source repo/pinned-commit-exact-match (MIRROR_SOURCE_COMMIT_PIN, dereferenced by the CLI via git show rather than trusted off the current worktree)/path/kind/schema_version checked, sha256 must equal the recomputed hash of the actual wrapper file at that commit (never the wrapper's self-report); the dereferenced wrapper's source_lock (including commit, not just repo/schema_version/season/row_count), declared forecast_mirror.paths (exact match, no missing/extra/substituted keys), authorized mirror filenames (live-directory hygiene check), and mirrored_hashes are all independently recomputed and matched
validator_enforcement.cutoff_validation: cutoff_at/cutoff_evidence_source null together or both present; non-null requires named reviewer/parseable reviewed_at, source_timezone_or_offset, and a published_draft_start_at that is itself hard-required to be a parseable offset-bearing instant; citation must reproduce (hash-verified); archive must state the season and contain the claimed published_draft_start_at; source_timezone_or_offset must agree with the offset actually embedded in published_draft_start_at; cutoff_at must be strictly earlier than that draft start
validator_enforcement.value_presence_agreement: availability_status is unavailable iff the real pinned mirror value for (player, field_family) is null, checked directly against the mirror JSON
validator_enforcement.official_postdraft_outcome_restriction: may never be eligible_at_cutoff -- definitionally post-draft information
validator_enforcement.unavailable_or_unresolved_row_shape: must carry null available_at and null evidence_source
eligible_or_ineligible_row_requirements: structurally complete evidence_source with non-empty mirrored_value_literal
reproduced archive actually contains the claimed literal
reproduced archive actually contains the claimed available_at itself, not just the value -- and not merely a bare date the artifact silently converted to an exact instant
claimed literal equals the REAL pinned mirror value for (player, field_family), recomputed directly from the committed mirror JSON
non-null review_decision with named reviewer and parseable reviewed_at
available_at correctly ordered against cutoff_at for the claimed status
validator_enforcement.population_accounting: exactly 240 distinct governed (identity, family) keys; every locked identity has all 5 families; no extra identity or family; declared status_counts and status_counts_by_family recomputed and must match, summing to 240/48 respectively
validator_enforcement.top_level_schema_closure: artifact top-level fields must match an exact enumerated set (AVAILABILITY_EVIDENCE_TOP_LEVEL_FIELDS); generated_at must be a parseable offset-bearing instant; generated_at_is_operational_timestamp_only_not_fact_availability must be exactly true; non-null source_snapshot_as_of must be a parseable offset-bearing instant (supplementary metadata only, never a substitute for available_at's archive-bound proof)

self_certification_hardening.when: during implementation, before this PR was reviewed
self_certification_hardening.gap_identified: mirrored_value_literal was validated only against the row's own cited archive, never against the real pinned mirror value -- the same self-certification shape Lane A's independent review took five rounds to close
self_certification_hardening.fix: MirrorVerificationContext gained mirrorValueLiterals, recomputed by the CLI directly from the committed mirror JSON (never trusted from the artifact or its cited archive); every eligible/ineligible row's mirrored_value_literal must now equal this real value exactly
self_certification_hardening.note: identified and closed by the implementing agent before any independent review occurred, applying the lesson already learned on Lane A rather than waiting for a review round to find it

post_review_strengthening_2026_07_13_aut.when: after PR #161 was opened, from an automated Codex review, before any human/repo-owner review

findings_addressed.gap_identified: available_at was compared to cutoff_at without ever being checked against the reproduced archive -- a row could self-declare an available_at that made it eligible while citing content that only proved the value existed, never that it was available on that date
findings_addressed.fix: the reproduced archive must now also contain the exact claimed available_at string before it is compared against cutoff_at; added regression tests for an undated archive and for a self-declared available_at the archive never states

findings_addressed.gap_identified: the dereferenced wrapper's source_lock comparison omitted commit, so a wrapper/artifact pair could in principle advance the pinned upstream TIBER-Rookies commit while keeping repo/schema_version/season/row_count and mirror hashes consistent, and the check would still report a match
findings_addressed.fix: the comparison now requires lock.commit === SOURCE_COMMIT as well; added a regression test tampering only source_lock.commit

post_review_strengthening_2026_07_13_own.when: after a full independent review by the repo owner on PR #161, before this PR merged
post_review_strengthening_2026_07_13_own.pr_lifecycle_correction: PR body changed from 'Closes #160' to 'Refs #160' and reframed as the Lane B scaffold plus unresolved baseline, not a claim that Lane B itself is complete -- issue #160 stays open after merge

findings_addressed.gap_identified: the automated pass's available_at-archive-binding fix was necessary but the owner requested the specific regression it should prevent: the same real, reproducing archive for a genuine eligible/ineligible snapshot paired with a DIFFERENT self-declared available_at that still lands on the same side of the cutoff, plus a source that only ever states a bare date being silently treated as midnight in some chosen timezone
findings_addressed.fix: added regression tests for the same-archive-different-timestamp-same-side case for both eligible and ineligible variants, and a bare-date-only archive case, all rejected via the existing archive-binding check (archive must contain the exact, fully-qualified available_at literal, not a derived approximation)

findings_addressed.gap_identified: published_draft_start_at was only checked for parseability inside the strict-ordering comparison, which simply skipped itself with no error when the value failed to parse (e.g. an archive stating 'not-a-timestamp'); source_timezone_or_offset was checked only for non-emptiness, never for agreement with the offset actually embedded in published_draft_start_at
findings_addressed.fix: published_draft_start_at is now hard-required to itself be a parseable offset-bearing instant as an independent check; source_timezone_or_offset must now agree with the offset extracted from published_draft_start_at (both normalized, Z treated as +00:00)

findings_addressed.gap_identified: (A) the wrapper source_lock comparison omitted commit -- addressed together with the automated-review P2 fix above; (B) the wrapper's declared forecast_mirror.paths were never checked, only the live directory's filenames and the recomputed content hashes, so a wrapper could declare a substituted or missing path entry and still pass; (C) mirror_source.commit was only shape-checked (looks like a 40-hex SHA), never dereferenced -- the CLI read wrapper/mirror bytes straight off the current worktree regardless of what commit the artifact claimed, so any other well-formed 40-hex value would still validate
findings_addressed.fix: (B) forecast_mirror.paths must now match the four authorized local paths exactly (no missing/extra/substituted keys); (C) mirror_source.commit is now checked against a single pinned exact value (MIRROR_SOURCE_COMMIT_PIN, the commit that merged Lane A, PR #159), and the CLI now dereferences the wrapper and all three mirror files at exactly that commit via `git show <commit>:<path>` rather than trusting the working tree to be at it -- the live mirror-directory listing remains a separate hygiene check against the real worktree

findings_addressed.gap_identified: rows had an exact-field check but the top-level artifact did not -- the committed JSON already carried generated_at_is_operational_timestamp_only_not_fact_availability, a field absent from the prior TypeScript contract and silently ignored by validation; generated_at and non-null source_snapshot_as_of/retrieved_at/reviewed_at were checked only as non-empty strings, not for their declared timestamp semantics
findings_addressed.fix: added AVAILABILITY_EVIDENCE_TOP_LEVEL_FIELDS as an exact, enumerated closed set for the artifact; generated_at must be a parseable offset-bearing instant; generated_at_is_operational_timestamp_only_not_fact_availability must be exactly true; non-null source_snapshot_as_of must be a parseable offset-bearing instant; retrieved_at (citations) and reviewed_at (cutoff evidence source, row review_decision) must be parseable dates, not merely non-empty strings

cutoff_section_8.detail: no archived, dated NFL draft-schedule evidence was assembled, and no human review of a cutoff candidate occurred during this implementation. cutoff_at and cutoff_evidence_source remain null.

per_row_exact_value_evidence_sections_10.detail: with no pinned cutoff, no row can honestly claim eligible_at_cutoff or ineligible_after_cutoff (both require a validly pinned cutoff_at as a hard precondition). no archived dated snapshot evidence was assembled for any of the 240 pairs.

missingness_vs_timing_section_11.detail: 17 (player, field_family) pairs carry a null value in the committed mirror itself -- a missingness fact, independent of any timing question. the remaining 223 pairs have a non-null pinned value but no availability-timing evidence, recorded unresolved_no_availability_proof.

accounting.unavailable_rows_are_exactly: the (player, field_family) pairs where the committed mirror's own value is null -- 1 player missing age_at_entry, 16 players missing athletic_testing; no other family has any missing value in the locked population

confirmations.no_human_review_claimed: review_decision is null on every row; cutoff_at/cutoff_evidence_source are null; no reviewer name or review date appears anywhere in the committed artifact

validation.tests: npm test -- full suite passes (1277 tests across the repo), including 76 in tests/rookieTransitionProfileAvailabilityEvidence.test.ts
test_coverage_summary: committed artifact passes validation against the real, pinned-commit-dereferenced mirror
kind/schema/design pins
exact 240-row population, no claimed human review, eight-field row contract
requires_followup decision and full accounting
missing identity (all five rows for a locked player removed)
missing single family for an otherwise-present identity
extra row for an unlocked identity
duplicate governed key
invalid field_family / availability_status tokens
extra or missing row field
out-of-order rows
tampered status_counts / status_counts_by_family
tampered issue reference and governing-design merge commit
value-presence agreement in both directions, with a real-null-value control case
official_postdraft_outcome rejected as eligible_at_cutoff even with otherwise-complete evidence
full cutoff-validation matrix including an unparseable published_draft_start_at, a source_timezone_or_offset/embedded-offset mismatch, and a clean control case
real-mirror-matched eligible_at_cutoff and ineligible_after_cutoff control cases
eligible claim with no cutoff pinned
unparseable available_at
missing evidence_source
empty mirrored_value_literal
non-reproducible row citation
archive that doesn't actually contain the claimed literal

archive-binding regressions: undated archive, self-declared available_at the archive never states, same-archive-different-timestamp-same-side-of-cutoff for both eligible and ineligible variants, and a bare-date-only archive (available_at may never be silently treated as midnight in a chosen timezone)
self-certification-gap regression: mirrored_value_literal the archive faithfully contains but that does not match the real pinned mirror value
missing or incomplete review_decision
unavailable/unresolved rows carrying a non-null available_at or evidence_source
timestamp-substitution rejections for both eligible_at_cutoff and ineligible_after_cutoff
a valid eligible row still only reaching requires_followup while other rows remain unresolved

mirror-wrapper dereferencing checks (tampered sha256, wrong/substituted-but-well-formed commit, wrong repo, mismatched recomputed hashes, unauthorized extra mirror file, tampered source_lock including a moved commit, and substituted/missing forecast_mirror.paths entries, with a control case)

top-level schema closure: extra/missing top-level field, unparseable generated_at, generated_at_is_operational_timestamp_only_not_fact_availability not exactly true, non-null source_snapshot_as_of that isn't a parseable instant, with a control case
malformed locked-population size
two inertness scans
validation.audit_cli: npm run audit:rookie-transition-profile-availability-evidence -- valid: true with the accounting above, now built by dereferencing the wrapper and mirror files at the exact pinned Forecast commit via git show

decision_rationale: The governed Lane B artifact, fail-closed validator, tests, and full 240-row accounting are in place and passing, but 223 of 240 (player, field_family) pairs remain unresolved_no_availability_proof: no pre-draft cutoff has been pinned (requires archived NFL draft-schedule evidence and an explicit, attributable human review, which has not occurred), and no archived exact-value snapshot evidence has been assembled for any pair.
decision_authorizes: a separate follow-up to pin a real, reviewed pre-draft cutoff
a separate follow-up to assemble archived, dated snapshot evidence per (player, field_family) pair, each with the required human review
